text:
Simone Biles looked a little sheepish after ‘forgetting’Olympicsprotocol before the gymnastics team final inParis.

Every team featuring in the women’s final received a superb reception as they made their way out to the Bercy Arena, but the volume was turned up a level for the introduction of Team USA.

Fans were understandably excited to welcome gymnastics superstar Biles back to the main stage, with the 27-year-old looking to add to her four Olympic gold medals after herTokyo 2020 nightmare.

After entering the arena, the gymnast finalists all stood in a line in front of their national flag before their names were individually read out and they addressed the fans with a smile and a wave.

Biles led USA out but forgot about this protocol and immediately went to take a seat on the side of the arena.

She was almost out of camera shot when her American team-mates shouted for her to come back.

Biles looked a little embarrassed as she realised her mistake and shared a joke with her team-mates before lining up for a deafening introduction.

‘She hasn’t been in the competition arena for a while and forgets to salute the crowd,’ BBC commentator Matt Baker said.

‘Simone, there’s a lot of people here to see you!’

Commonwealth Games gold-winning gymnast Craig Heap added: ‘I think she’s just keen to get on with the competition!

Slay ð«Simone Biles makes the vault look easy in the women’s all-round team final.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/hIiS87n1gi

‘That proves how much she wants it. This USA team is absolutely remarkable and the title is theirs to throw away in my opinion.’

Biles won all four of her Olympic gold medals at Rio 2016 and was expected to add to her tally in Tokyo three years ago.

But the American pulled out of four finals after suffering with the ‘twisties’.

Biles looked back to her best in the Paris 2024 artistic gymnastics on Sunday and made a superb start to this evening’s team final.

She recorded a 14.900 in the vault table – the same apparatus that prompted her difficulties at Tokyo – and also impressed on the bars for Team USA.

‘Simone Biles is absolutely fizzing with energy,’ BBC Radio 5 Live commentator Eleanor Oldroyd said after Biles’ first two efforts in the final.

‘We were a little worried [heading into this final] because she had a little calf issue in qualifying but no sign of that now. High fives from her team-mates.’

title: Simone Biles embarrassed after 'forgetting' Olympics protocol | Metro News
description: Simone Biles looked a little sheepish after 'forgetting' Olympics protocol before the gymnastics team final in Paris.
